title: [BUG] File won't be saved properly (read&write)
author: theMasix

comments.author: Alanscut
comments.body: @theMasix Can you provide the `input.xlsx`? This is helpful for us to solve the problem 😄.

comments.author: theMasix
comments.body: @Alanscut It could be any ```xlsx``` file. I tested it with many files Including empty file even! The output couldn't be opened anyway.

comments.author: Alanscut
theMasix
comments.body:
@Alanscut Finally I figured out what the issue was!
All columns and rows were hidden after saving and strangely in this situation, the excel couldn't be opened too!
But if I add the visibility to each row and col manually, the file would open too!
So the problem is solved now.
But I don't know why all rows and cols were hidden after saving.

comments.author: Alanscut
comments.body: @theMasix Can you please provide the test code and the version of miscosoft excel, I did not reproduce this error.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
Info: issue originally started on stack overflow: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61922359/exceljs-doesnt-save-file-properly/61953353?noredirect=1

@Alanscut Exceljs saved some information in a different way than Excel. It's hard to follow it.
I think the most sensible solution to track these differences is unzip Input and Output files and make a diff between two folders.

BTW. I had a similar issue with images, fixed in #702

comments.author: theMasix
theMasix
